creator: obrien@FreeBSD.org
text:
I just put up https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-ports/pull/259, not knowing I'm not the first to want this port's behavior changed.

Users should not be subjected to needless warnings:

$ egrep COPYRIGHT /COPYRIGHT
egrep: warning: egrep is obsolescent; using /usr/local/bin/ggrep -E
#       @(#)COPYRIGHT   8.2 (Berkeley) 3/21/94

$ fgrep COPYRIGHT /COPYRIGHT
fgrep: warning: fgrep is obsolescent; using /usr/local/bin/ggrep -F
#       @(#)COPYRIGHT   8.2 (Berkeley) 3/21/94
When they want to use the traditional [ef]grep short-cuts. More so this can cause problems in scripts when /usr/local is ahead of /usr/bin in one's path.

diff --git textproc/gnugrep/Makefile textproc/gnugrep/Makefile
index 12e118..fc8b9f 100644
--- textproc/gnugrep/Makefile
+++ textproc/gnugrep/Makefile
@@ -22,7 +22,7 @@ INFO=         grep
 CPE_VENDOR=    gnu
 
 OPTIONS_DEFINE=                COMPAT ICONV LIBSIGSEGV NLS PCRE
-OPTIONS_DEFAULT=       COMPAT ICONV PCRE
+OPTIONS_DEFAULT=       ICONV PCRE
 COMPAT_DESC=           Create g-prefixless compatibility symlinks
 LIBSIGSEGV_DESC=       Use alternative page fault handler (GNU libsigsegv)
 OPTIONS_SUB=           yes
